description: TIUTH JUSTICE The United States has no desire to interfere in European affairs or to act as arbiter in European territorial disputes PRESIDENT WILSON TO THE CONGRESS FEBRUARY 11 1918 How Japan Is Spreading Out Upon the Mainland of Asia u y j SCAL Q MLES s I r SN gHmN 3QO Waf lUSIU Map showing Japan and Japanese accessions on the Asiatic continent The shaded area is now in control of Japan ALL reports agree that Japan has re ceived full recognition of her claims to Shantung Province in China The secret treaty made in the darkest days of the war between Japan Great Britain France and Russia has thus been carried out President Wilson was not informed even of the existence of this secret treaty until Japans claim came before the Peace Con ference a fortnight ago China protested in vain Japan pledges her honor before the Peace Conference to restore in her own time Chinese sovereignty over the former German fortress and leasehold of Kiaochau but is to retain police authority in Shantung its railroads and all mining rights This was one of the demands China re fused to accede to under compulsion of Japan in 1915
